name: Ex Vivo Mitochondrial Transfer
summary: Cell-therapy style mitochondrial transfer performed outside the body before cellular testing or reinfusion.
mechanism_summary: The approach transfers isolated mitochondria into recipient cells ex vivo, with the goal of improving bioenergetic state, activation capacity, differentiation, or senescence-associated phenotypes before any therapeutic cell use.

open_questions: Whether ex vivo mitochondrial transfer can generate durable in vivo benefit after cell delivery
Which elderly-human cell types are viable therapeutic targets
Whether improved cell biomarkers translate to clinically meaningful immune or tissue function
How donor mitochondria source, compatibility, and quality control should be standardized
